meta.instructions: Edit this file to add, update or remove contacts. Commit and push to GitHub. Netlify auto-deploys in under 60 seconds. Use the + Add button in the app for quick adds, then commit this file to make them permanent.

contacts.name: Bradley Mogere

contacts.why: Medical supply chain. Operations partner. Active revenue source. Core trust anchor for supply business.
contacts.know: Long-standing relationship. Know his working style, motivations, and reliability track record. Works well with Melchi.
contacts.knows: Hospital procurement officers, medical wholesalers Nairobi, distribution contacts
contacts.favours: Equal partners — mutual value. Owe: written partnership agreement by May 2026.
contacts.nextAction: Monthly call this week. Consignment count, margin review, partnership agreement discussion.

contacts.met: Existing business relationship — co-partner in supply venture
contacts.why: Supply chain partner. Operational logistics role. Third partner in supply venture.
contacts.know: Active partner. Works well with Bradley. Logistics-focused approach.
contacts.knows: Medical distributors, hospital contacts
contacts.favours: Equal partner. Owe: formalised written agreement, transparent financials.
contacts.nextAction: 3-way call with Bradley this month. Formalise written partnership agreement.

contacts.name: Sandrah
contacts.met: Life
contacts.why: Life partner. Co-emigration planner. Fellow physician. B1/B2 visa applicant. Future mother of our children.
contacts.know: Everything. Petname: daddy shark. Graduating 2027. Diplomatic passport history. Extensive travel record.
contacts.knows: MTRH clinical network, her faculty contacts, family connections, diplomatic contacts
contacts.favours: She gives everything. Nails every 2 weeks. Monthly surprise. Weekly date night pre-booked. Always.
contacts.nextAction: Plan April getaway NOW — book before exam week. B1/B2 docs together this weekend.

contacts.role: Internal Medicine & Paediatrics — Indiana University / AMPATH MTRH
contacts.met: AMPATH Fireside Wednesday — MTRH

contacts.knows: IU Internal Medicine and Paediatrics program directors, other AMPATH faculty
contacts.favours: Shared MTRH SHA claims process insight. Offer: digital systems tour, Uzimatek demo.

contacts.favours: Pitched at their event — added credibility. Offer: quarterly Uzimatek updates, co-create with their team.

contacts.met: Medical school — clinical faculty across rotations
contacts.why: Dean's letter / MSPE for ERAS application. Clinical reference letter. Internal academic ally.

contacts.knows: MU Faculty network, MTRH administration, East Africa medical education leadership

contacts.name: Dr. Tony Nguyen
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 1 Anaesthesiology attachment
contacts.why: LOR potential for ECFMG/ERAS. Direct anaesthesia mentor — directly relevant to current MTRH anaesthesia rotation. Described OR safety culture as 'military-like'. Deep expertise in perioperative systems and closed-loop communication.
contacts.know: Very structured and systems-focused. Described KP's OR culture explicitly. Narrated EPIC EHR's real-time anaesthetic logging. Strong teacher energy.
contacts.knows: KP anaesthesiology department, OR safety faculty, perioperative medicine network
contacts.favours: Gave me 1 week of structured OR exposure. I owe him a meaningful update on my MTRH anaesthesia rotation progress.
contacts.nextAction: Email referencing the 'military-like OR culture' insight — directly relevant to current anaesthesia rotation. MTRH comparison. Ask about 2028 observership.

contacts.name: Dr. Jennifer Nguyen
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 2 Diagnostic Cardiology / TEE
contacts.why: Cardiology is my target specialty. Supervised TEE — modality unavailable at MTRH. Direct connection to KP cardiology. LOR potential.
contacts.know: Supervised TEE procedure for valvular assessment and LAA visualisation. Calm, precise, excellent teacher. Connection to structural heart disease team.
contacts.knows: KP cardiology department, structural heart disease team, TAVR programme
contacts.favours: Gave me direct TEE exposure — rare privilege for a medical student. I owe a progress update on how this shaped my cardiology ambitions.
contacts.nextAction: Email: reference TEE session and how it contextualised TAVR. Express cardiology career interest. Ask about 2028 observership.

contacts.name: Dr. Leong
contacts.role: Interventional Cardiologist — Kaiser Permanente Cath Lab
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 3 Cardiac Catheterisation Laboratory
contacts.why: HIGHEST PRIORITY KP contact. Interventional cardiology is my target sub-specialty. Let me scrub in for a PCI — stood at table for wire navigation and stent deployment. LOR from an interventional cardiologist = gold for cardiology fellowship.
contacts.know: Narrated every PCI step. Invited questions throughout. Explained fluoroscopy anatomy in real time. Generous with access — scrub in was unusual for a visiting student.
contacts.knows: KP interventional cardiology team, cath lab network, structural heart disease programme (TAVR team)
contacts.favours: Let me scrub into a PCI. Extraordinary privilege for a visiting international student. I owe a detailed, thoughtful thank-you update.
contacts.nextAction: EMAIL PRIORITY 1. Reference scrubbing into PCI — specific wire navigation moment. Ask directly: would he support a 2028 interventional cardiology observership?

contacts.name: Dr. Howard
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 3 Cardiac Care Unit
contacts.why: Asked me to formally present a case (75yo AV block) — assessed my clinical performance directly. LOR potential. CCU exposure relevant for IM residency and cardiology fellowship.
contacts.know: Discussed pharmacogenomics — hydralazine-nitrate for African American HF patients (BiDil trial data). Equity-conscious prescriber. High standards.
contacts.knows: KP CCU team, inpatient cardiology network, heart failure programme
contacts.favours: Gave me rare opportunity of a formal CCU case presentation. I owe a substantive clinical update.
contacts.nextAction: Email referencing AV block case presentation. Note how it deepened inpatient cardiology interest. Update on MTRH cardiology exposure.

contacts.name: Dr. Kohan
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 3 Cardiac Care Unit (alongside Dr. Howard)
contacts.why: Co-supervising attending in CCU. ACS, decompensated HF, complex arrhythmias, post-procedural monitoring exposure. LOR potential as CCU co-attending.
contacts.know: Co-attending with Dr. Howard. Part of team that supervised my formal case presentation.
contacts.knows: KP CCU team, inpatient cardiology
contacts.favours: Supervised my CCU rotation. Professional thank-you and update owed.
contacts.nextAction: Email alongside Dr. Howard outreach. Brief, professional. Reference CCU experience and cardiology interest.

contacts.name: Dr. Doris
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 5 Electrophysiology Laboratory
contacts.why: EP is a cardiology sub-specialty — directly relevant to fellowship pathway. Supervised WPW ablation and AF ablation using CARTO 3D electroanatomical mapping. Rare procedural exposure. LOR potential.
contacts.know: Ran ECG interpretation clinic before EP lab — systematic review of complex arrhythmias. Thorough teacher. Supervised two ablation procedures.
contacts.knows: KP electrophysiology department, arrhythmia network, CARTO mapping specialists
contacts.favours: Gave me EP lab access and dedicated ECG teaching session. Substantive clinical update owed.
contacts.nextAction: Email referencing WPW ablation and CARTO mapping. Express interest in EP as possible cardiology subspecialty.

contacts.name: Dr. Puente
contacts.role: Paediatrician — Kaiser Permanente Baldwins Hills Crenshaw / Inglewood
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Weeks 1 and 4 Paediatrics outpatient clinic
contacts.why: Saw me across two separate weeks — rare continuity. Modelled motivational interviewing for vaccine hesitancy and adolescent HEADSS framework. LOR potential given repeated exposure.
contacts.know: Handles ethnically diverse catchment. Extremely patient with vaccine-hesitant families. Evidence-based communication style. Warm and approachable.
contacts.knows: KP paediatrics department, community health outreach network
contacts.favours: Welcomed me back for Week 4 — continuity is a mark of trust. Warm personal thank-you owed.
contacts.nextAction: Personal email — reference HEADSS framework and how I've started using it at MTRH.

contacts.name: Dr. Caivano
contacts.role: Haematologist / Sickle Cell Specialist — Kaiser Permanente West Los Angeles
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 1 Sickle Cell Service
contacts.why: SCD is one of the most common haematological diagnoses at MTRH. Showed me comprehensive SCD management (voxelotor, crizanlizumab, PCA pumps, MDT). Huge relevance for Uzimatek data and global health research angle.
contacts.know: Runs multidisciplinary SCD service. Gap between KP and MTRH SCD management was the starkest clinical contrast of the rotation. PCA pump approach, social worker integration.
contacts.knows: KP haematology department, SCD research network, global health medicine contacts
contacts.favours: Gave me SCD clinic access — deeply relevant to MTRH experience. Reflective update owed.
contacts.nextAction: Email referencing SCD life expectancy contrast (5 years in SSA vs 50+ years at KP). Mention Uzimatek — this data gap drives health informatics in Africa.

contacts.name: Dr. Ethnasios
contacts.role: Internist — Kaiser Permanente Outpatient / Primary Care
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Weeks 2 and 4 Internal Medicine outpatient
contacts.why: Internal Medicine is my US residency pathway. Supervised IM outpatient across two weeks — direct assessment of my clinical skills. LOR potential. Communication approach (motivational interviewing, obesity counselling) was a specific learning point.
contacts.know: Warm, methodical teacher. Modelled evidence-based communication as a practised skill. Childhood obesity and vaccine hesitancy handled across two sessions.
contacts.knows: KP internal medicine department, primary care network, outpatient medicine faculty
contacts.favours: Supervised IM outpatient across two separate weeks. Clinical update and thank-you owed.
contacts.nextAction: Email: IM is my US residency target. Reference IM outpatient sessions. Ask about IMG preparation for IM residency.

contacts.name: Dr. Parekh
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 2 Urological Surgery (DaVinci)
contacts.why: Witnessed robotic partial nephrectomy and radical prostatectomy. Surgical innovation relevant to Uzimatek's health system narrative. LOR potential.
contacts.know: Operates DaVinci. 90% of cases managed as same-day or 23-hour procedures. EBL consistently under 100mL. Technically precise.
contacts.knows: KP urology department, robotic surgery programme
contacts.favours: Gave me OR access for robotic surgery. Professional thank-you owed.
contacts.nextAction: Brief professional email. Reference DaVinci partial nephrectomy — the 7-degree wrist articulation. Warm, concise.

contacts.name: Dr. Tammadon
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 2 Urological Surgery (DaVinci), alongside Dr. Parekh
contacts.why: Co-operating surgeon on DaVinci robotic cases. LOR potential alongside Dr. Parekh.
contacts.know: Co-surgeon on robotic urology cases with Dr. Parekh.
contacts.knows: KP urology department, robotic surgery programme
contacts.favours: OR access during robotic surgery. Professional thank-you owed.
contacts.nextAction: Brief professional email alongside Dr. Parekh outreach.

contacts.name: Dr. Chi
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 4 Gender-Affirming Surgery (7-hour vaginoplasty)
contacts.why: One of the most surgically complex cases of the rotation — 7-hour penile inversion vaginoplasty. Demonstrated multidisciplinary surgical care. Unique exposure. LOR potential from specialised surgical environment.
contacts.know: Performs complex gender-affirming surgery. Meticulous neurovascular dissection. Patient journey spans 12–24 months referral to post-op rehab.
contacts.knows: KP plastic surgery department, gender-affirming surgery programme, multidisciplinary surgical teams
contacts.favours: Allowed observation of a 7-hour complex surgical case. Professional thank-you owed.
contacts.nextAction: Brief thoughtful email. Reference multidisciplinary care pathway (psychiatry, endocrinology, physio, gynaecology) as a systems-thinking lesson.

contacts.name: Dr. Diego
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 5 Interventional Radiology
contacts.why: Supervised UAE, G-tube placement, and port placement. IR relevant to Uzimatek data capture narrative. Unique exposure. LOR potential.
contacts.know: Performed UAE, G-tube placement, and port placement. IR as surgical alternative — relevant to resource-limited settings.
contacts.knows: KP interventional radiology department, IR network
contacts.favours: Gave me IR lab access. Professional thank-you owed.
contacts.nextAction: Email referencing UAE as minimally invasive alternative to surgery. Note IR resource implications vs MTRH — Uzimatek-relevant insight.

contacts.name: Dr. Cara
contacts.met: 2025 KP Rotation — Week 5 Urgent Care (final day of rotation)
contacts.why: Full-spectrum urgent care exposure on final day. KP urgent care as system pressure valve — preventing ED overcrowding. Relevant to MTRH design recommendations in electives report.
contacts.know: Final day of rotation. High-volume, fast-paced urgent care. Demonstrated how KP urgent care reduces ED burden.
contacts.knows: KP urgent care network, emergency medicine contacts
contacts.favours: Gave me final-day clinical exposure. Brief professional thank-you owed.
contacts.nextAction: Brief thank-you email. Reference urgent care as ED pressure valve insight — influenced MTRH recommendations in electives report.
